commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then the chiefs of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab, trembling shall take hold upon them; all the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away. Stiff, with consternation. See Josue ix. The nations of Chanaan found auxiliaries even among the near relations of the Hebrews, the children of Esau, (who were not governed by princes, Alp him, as in Genesis xxxvi. ) and of Lot. We easily forget our relations, when our interest is at stake. Hebrew, instead of being stiff, says, they "melted away. "Both words insinuate, that their heart was under such a violent struggle, that they could perform no duty.
